No it was purely about money. The salary cap rules at that time did not allow Cavs to go over the cap to resign him (because Boozer was a second round pick). So they let him out of the contract a year early, with the idea he would take the below market extension in exchange for being let out early. Instead he signed a huge contract (for the times) with the Jazz, and Cavs could not match because it would put them over the cap.
